item.title: How the Iran War Is Disrupting Global Oil and Gas Supply
item.description: Oil and gas markets have been rocked by disruption to supply out of the Persian Gulf. Prices of both commodities have jumped since the US and Israel began their strikes on Iran.

item.title: Why Diesel Prices Are the Real Concern for the Economy
item.description: No one can stop talking about sky-high gasoline prices. The ripple effects of diesel are worth watching too.

item.title: Oil Tanker Market’s Key Rate Thrown Into Chaos With Hormuz Shut
item.description: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has thrown a vital part of the shipping market into chaos.

item.title: Nexstar Plans $5.1 Billion Bond Sale for Tegna Deal, Lowers Loan
item.description: Nexstar Media Group Inc. plans to sell $5.12 billion of bonds to help fund the acquisition of fellow TV-station owner Tegna Inc., marking a shift in the financing plans for the deal.

item.title: ECB Asks Banks How Iran War Is Impacting Clients, Operations
item.description: The European Central Bank is asking lenders about fallout from the Iran war on their operations and clients, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Iran War’s Energy Shock Puts Some Major IMF Borrowers at Risk
item.description: Conflict in the Middle East is sending shockwaves across the developing world, raising the prospect that emerging economies will turn to the International Monetary Fund for more help.

item.title: xAI Sends Engineers to Client Sites to Win Business from OpenAI
item.description: Elon Musk’s xAI is sending engineers directly into the offices of prospective corporate clients in an attempt to claw business customers away from rivals OpenAI and Anthropic PBC.

item.title: Bowman Still Projecting Three Interest-Rate Cuts Before Year-End
item.description: The Federal Reserve’s top bank cop still supports three interest-rate cuts in 2026 and said she expects strong economic growth this year but is keeping an eye on the impact on the war in Iran.

item.title: White House Unveils AI Legislative Plan for Skeptical Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump released a national framework for regulating artificial intelligence on Friday, laying the groundwork for Congress to create a federal standard for the rapidly growing technology.

item.title: Fed’s Waller Cautious On Oil, May Advocate Rate Cuts Later
item.description: Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller said he is cautious about how surging oil prices on the back of the Iran war will impact inflation, though a weak job market may still call for interest-rate cuts later this year.

item.title: Meta, Alphabet Join Credit-Risk Index as AI Hedging Demand Soars
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc., Alphabet Inc. and Microsoft Corp. joining an index of high-grade firms’ credit default swaps is another sign of investors increasingly hedging hyperscalers’ debt amid surging bond sales.

item.title: Irish Economy in Better Position Than European Peers on Iran War Risk, Makhlouf Says
item.description: Ireland is better placed than many of its European peers to weather economic risks from the Iran war fallout as it starts from a stronger base, Central Bank of Ireland Governor Gabriel Makhlouf said.

item.title: Ecolab to Buy Data Center Cooling Firm CoolIT From KKR Funds
item.description: Ecolab Inc. has agreed to buy CoolIT Systems Inc., a company that develops cooling technology for AI data centers, in a $4.75 billion all-cash deal.

item.title: Canadian Consumer Spending Rose Ahead of Oil Price Shock
item.description: Canadian retail sales kept rising ahead of an oil price shock that may divert spending away from non-essential items.

item.title: BOE’s Messaging Is Under Fire as Hawkish Pivot Fuels Bond Rout
item.description: The Bank of England’s revamp of its communication policy to include the views of individual rate setters is getting criticized by market analysts on concerns it helped fuel a selloff in UK bonds.

item.title: Drone Tech Maker’s 1,000% Surge Shows Latest Wall Street Fad
item.description: The eye-popping market debut of a tiny drone software company this week reveals a newfound investor appetite for stocks that fall at the intersection of geopolitics, rising spending on defense technology and artificial intelligence.

item.title: Chinese Robot Maker Unitree Seeks $610 Million in Shanghai IPO
item.description: China’s leading robot company, Unitree Robotics, has filed for an initial public offering in Shanghai, moving a step closer to a domestic listing as investor interest in the sector builds.

item.title: US Stock Futures Drop as Traders Eye Longer War, Options Expiry
item.description: US futures extended a drop on Friday as earlier hopes for a quick resolution to the war in the Middle East faded. Meanwhile, traders braced for a historic amount of March options expiry.

item.title: A 20% Stock Rally Makes Ghana World’s Top Performer Since War
item.description: A scorching rally by Ghana’s main stock index since the start of the Iran war tops a small cohort of equity markets that have gained amid a wider global selloff.

item.title: De Beers Makes Sweeping Cuts to Its Elite Diamond-Buying Club
item.description: De Beers has made one of the deepest ever cuts to its group of handpicked diamond buyers as the former monopoly grapples with a prolonged crisis roiling the industry, people with knowledge of the matter said.

item.title: US Premarket Movers: FedEx, Planet Labs, Super Micro, York Space
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures are down 0.5% as of 7:50 a.m. in New York. Iran pressed ahead with attacks on Gulf Arab states even after Israel signaled it would stop targeting the Islamic Republic’s energy infrastructure.

item.title: Removing Sanctions on Iran’s Crude Isn’t an Easy Fix for Oil Prices
item.description: The potential lifting of US restrictions points to the White House’s scattergun approach in trying to bring down prices at the pump.

item.title: SAP CEO Says Defense Industry Sales Are Fastest Growing Business
item.description: SAP SE Chief Executive Officer Christian Klein said the defense industry has become the company’s fastest growing business line as military budgets soar around the world.

item.title: Feast-or-Famine Credit Markets Lure Bargain Hunters to New Sales
item.description: Credit investors are scoring some of the highest new issue concessions in years. That’s because companies that seize the occasional window for bond offerings are competing to entice buyers who want to be compensated for a growing list of risks.

item.title: Italy Joins Race for Algerian Gas With Iran War Cutting Supplies
item.description: Italy is in talks with Algeria to buy more natural gas from the North African nation with the Iran war squeezing energy shipments, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Zegna Chairman Says War Has Made Luxury Demand Uncertain
item.description: Italian fashion house Ermenegildo Zegna cautioned that the war in the Middle East has reduced visibility on luxury demand, while China, the US and Europe were holding up.

item.title: Algebris Ramps Up Cash, Warning of Further Disruptions From War
item.description: Algebris Investments, a heavyweight in European bank debt, has increased cash levels in its credit funds to some of their highest levels ever, warning that markets are underplaying risks related to the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Trump Just Shook Up Global Interest-Rate Policy Again
item.description: The US president’s capacity to unnerve central bankers is as untrammeled as ever

item.title: Nvidia CEO Finds It Harder to Wow His Audience
item.description: Huang doesn’t always feel like he’s getting his message across

item.title: Orange Plans to Name Director Frederic Sanchez As Next Chairman
item.description: Orange SA is close to naming director Frédéric Sanchez as its next chairman as the French carrier weighs a major deal at home, according to people familiar with the matter.
